read.image: outside the glass, watching him bleed in it
read.take: She did everything right. She saw him clearly, watched him come apart, got herself out and was glad of it. None of that is the problem. The problem is that being right about him does nothing to make her want him less. So she names it: a sickness, the kind that has you begging someone to roll the windows down. She says she is trying to stay clean. Then, almost quietly, she wonders what would happen if she just gave in.
read.contradiction: She can hardly feel anything anymore, and the one thing she can still feel is the pull back toward him.

arc.label: The Reckoning
arc.scene: She hates him for what he did and misses him anyway, and she cannot argue herself out of either one. The two feelings do not take turns. They sit on top of each other until she can barely tell them apart.

arc.label: The Sickness
arc.scene: She finally has a name for the feeling. It moves like motion sickness, a floor that will not hold still and a body that turns against her. She could scream until her throat gave out and still not find a word in any language big enough to get him out of her, and the sickness just keeps turning with nowhere to land.

arc.label: The Way Out
arc.scene: From the safe side of it, she can finally see him for what he is, a man who throws rocks around his own room and lies bleeding in what is left of it. She got out, and she is glad of it. She is also, to her own surprise, sorry it came to this for him, and sorry the whole thing went down the way it did.

arc.label: The Pull Back
arc.scene: He was bored when they met, already grown and in a band the year she was born, and all of that should have been enough to keep her gone. It never quite is. The pull toward him runs under everything like a low sound she cannot tune out, and after all the leaving she has done, she still catches herself listening for it, still unwilling to promise she would not go back.

lines.line:
I hate you for what you did
And I miss you like a little kid

I'll be glad that I made it out
And sorry that it all went down like it did
You were in a band when I was born

I wanna know what would happen
If I surrender to the sound

read.texture: Acoustic indie folk, quiet and unhurried, more danceable than its mood. The sound runs brighter than the words, an upbeat surface over everything that would rather lie down.
